source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A frame of a rectangular picture is made from planks of equal width. What is the width of these planks (in centimetres) if the outside perimeter of the frame is $8 \mathrm{~cm}$ more than the inside perimeter?
<image1>

solving typically involves at least two linked steps: expressing how width changes both rectangle dimensions, then using the perimeter difference to solve for the width
answer_type is numeric with no choices, so the solver must derive rather than select
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value
the claimed answer can be checked by a deterministic perimeter relation for a rectangular frame
